about.body:
Vauchi — Privacy-focused updatable contact cards.

Version 0.5.0
License: GPL-3.0-or-later
about.what_is_vauchi.body: Vauchi is a privacy-focused contact card app. You exchange cards in person via QR code. Your data is encrypted end-to-end and never stored on our servers. When you update your card, all your contacts receive the update automatically.
about.what_is_vauchi.title: What is Vauchi?

faq.block_contact: How do I block a contact?
faq.block_contact.answer:
Yes. Blocking a contact:

1. Removes them from your contact list
2. Stops sending them your updates
3. Ignores any updates from them

To block: Tap the contact > Menu > Block Contact

They won't be notified that they're blocked.
faq.block_contact.question: Can I block someone?
faq.data_storage.answer:
Your data lives in three places:

**On your device:**
- Your identity (private keys)
- Your contact card
- Your contacts' cards
- All settings

**On the relay server (encrypted):**
- Messages waiting to be delivered
- Never readable by us

**Nowhere else.**

We never have access to your unencrypted data.
faq.data_storage.question: What data is stored where?
faq.encryption.answer:
Vauchi uses multiple layers of encryption:

**End-to-end encryption:**
Your data is encrypted on your device before it leaves. Only you and your intended recipients can read it.

**Double Ratchet protocol:**
Each message uses a unique key. Even if one key is compromised, other messages remain secure.

**Local storage encryption:**
Data on your device is encrypted with your device key.

We use the same cryptographic standards as Signal.
faq.encryption.question: How is my data protected?
faq.first_contact: How do I add my first contact?
faq.first_contact.answer:
To add a contact, you need to meet in person:

1. Tap the Exchange tab
2. Show your QR code to the other person
3. They scan it with their Vauchi app
4. You scan their QR code
5. Done! You're connected.

This in-person requirement prevents spam and ensures you only connect with people you've actually met.
faq.first_contact.question: How do I add my first contact?
faq.how_updates_work.answer:
When you edit your card:

1. The change is encrypted on your device
2. Sent to the relay server (still encrypted)
3. Delivered to your contacts' devices
4. Decrypted only on their devices

Updates are typically delivered within seconds. If a contact is offline, updates wait on the relay and are delivered when they reconnect.
faq.how_updates_work.question: How do updates reach my contacts?
faq.multiple_devices: Can I use Vauchi on multiple devices?
faq.multiple_devices.answer:
Yes! Link your devices in Settings:

1. On your new device, install Vauchi
2. Choose 'Link to Existing Identity'
3. On your original device, go to Settings > Devices > Link Device
4. Scan the QR code on your new device

Your contacts, cards, and settings will sync across devices.
faq.multiple_devices.question: Can I use Vauchi on multiple devices?
faq.offline_updates: What happens when a contact is offline?
faq.offline_updates.answer:
Vauchi handles offline gracefully:

**When you go offline:**
- Your edits are saved locally
- They'll be sent when you reconnect

**When contacts are offline:**
- Updates queue on the relay
- Delivered when they reconnect
- No data loss

The relay stores encrypted messages for up to 30 days.
faq.offline_updates.question: What happens when I'm offline?
faq.phone_lost: What if I lose my phone?
faq.phone_lost.answer:
Your identity can be recovered through Social Recovery. If you've set up recovery contacts, meet 3 or more of them in person. They can vouch for you, and your identity will be restored.

To set up recovery: Go to Settings > Recovery > Add Recovery Contacts.
faq.phone_lost.question: What happens if I lose my phone?
faq.recovery_setup: How do I set up social recovery?
faq.recovery_setup.answer:
Recovery contacts are people who can vouch for your identity if you lose access:

1. Go to Settings > Recovery
2. Tap 'Add Recovery Contact'
3. Meet your trusted contact in person
4. They'll scan your QR code to become a recovery contact

We recommend adding 5-7 people you trust and see regularly.
faq.recovery_setup.question: How do I set up recovery contacts?
faq.remove_contact: How do I remove a contact?
faq.remove_contact.answer:
To remove a contact:

1. Go to your Contacts list
2. Tap on the contact
3. Tap the menu (three dots)
4. Select 'Remove Contact'

Note: They will still have your card, but won't receive your future updates. Consider asking them to remove you too.
faq.remove_contact.question: How do I remove a contact?
faq.tracking: Can anyone track me through Vauchi?
faq.tracking.answer:
No. Vauchi is designed with privacy first:

- No location data is ever collected or shared
- Your card contains only what you choose to add
- All data is end-to-end encrypted
- The relay server can't read your content
- We have no analytics or tracking

Your contacts only see the fields you've shared with them.
faq.tracking.question: Can someone track me through Vauchi?
faq.visibility_default: What information is shared by default?
faq.visibility_default.answer:
By default, all fields are visible to all contacts.

To restrict a field:
1. Edit your card
2. Tap the field
3. Tap 'Visibility'
4. Choose which labels can see it

You can also set per-contact overrides for fine-grained control.
faq.visibility_default.question: What's the default visibility?
faq.visibility_labels.answer:
Visibility labels let you control who sees what:

1. Create labels like 'Family', 'Work', 'Friends'
2. Assign contacts to labels
3. Mark fields as visible to specific labels

Example: Your home address shows to 'Family' but not 'Work'.

To set up: Go to your card > tap a field > Set Visibility.
faq.visibility_labels.question: What are visibility labels?
faq.why_in_person: Why do I need to exchange in person?
faq.why_in_person.answer:
The in-person QR exchange is a core privacy feature:

- **No spam**: You can't be added by strangers
- **Verified identity**: You know who you're connecting with
- **No social graph**: We can't see who you know
- **Physical trust**: Cryptographic trust starts with physical presence

This is similar to how Signal verifies contacts in person.
faq.why_in_person.question: Why do I need to meet in person?

info.auto_lock.body: When enabled, your app will automatically lock after a period of inactivity. You'll need to re-authenticate to access your data.
info.auto_lock.title: Auto-Lock
info.backup_password.body: Your backup password encrypts your data before it leaves your device. Without it, your backup cannot be restored. Store it somewhere safe — we cannot recover it for you.
info.backup_password.title: Backup Password
info.contact_info_optional.body: All contact fields are optional. You choose what to share and with whom. Fields you leave empty are never sent to your contacts.
info.contact_info_optional.title: Optional Contact Info
info.duress_pin.body: A duress PIN triggers an emergency shred of your data if entered instead of your regular PIN. Use this if you're forced to unlock your device under coercion.
info.duress_pin.title: Duress PIN
info.groups_purpose.body: Groups let you organize your contacts and control what information each group can see. When you add a contact to a group, they only see fields marked as visible for that group.
info.groups_purpose.title: Groups
info.recovery_trust.body: Recovery trustees are contacts you trust to help you regain access to your identity if you lose your device. They hold encrypted recovery shards that can only be combined with your password.
info.recovery_trust.title: Recovery Trust
info.visibility_meaning.body: Visibility controls which contacts can see each of your fields. By default, new fields are visible to everyone. Use labels to restrict visibility to specific groups.
info.visibility_meaning.title: Visibility

link_exchange.contact_added_subtitle: The other person's contact card has been saved.
link_exchange.link_failed_subtitle: The contact exchange could not be completed.
link_exchange.reason_decrypt_error: The received card could not be decrypted. Please try again.
link_exchange.reason_deposit_rejected: The relay rejected the exchange. Please try sharing the link again.
link_exchange.reason_generic: Something went wrong completing the exchange. Please try again.
link_exchange.reason_handshake_failed: The secure handshake failed. Please share a fresh link.
link_exchange.reason_polling_timed_out: The other device did not respond in time. Share the link again to retry.
link_exchange.retrieving_a11y_hint: Fetching and decrypting the contact card
link_exchange.share_link_subtitle: Send this link to exchange contacts
link_exchange.success_detail: You can find the new contact in your contacts list.
link_exchange.waiting_a11y_hint: The other person needs to open the link to complete the exchange
link_exchange.waiting_detail: They need to open the link to complete the exchange.
link_exchange.waiting_subtitle: The link has been shared. Waiting for the other person...
link_responder.completed_detail: You can find the new contact in your contacts list.
link_responder.completed_subtitle: The sender's contact card has been saved.
link_responder.failed_subtitle: The contact card could not be received.
link_responder.failure_decrypt_error: The received card could not be decrypted. Please try again.
link_responder.failure_generic: Something went wrong receiving the contact card. Please try again.
link_responder.failure_rejected: The relay rejected the exchange. Please try sharing the link again.
link_responder.failure_timed_out: The other device did not respond in time. Ask them to share the link again.
link_responder.waiting_a11y_hint: The sender's encrypted card will be retrieved when both sides' deposits have matched on the relay
link_responder.waiting_detail: The sender's contact card will appear here once the relay has matched both deposits.
lock.a11y_hint: Authenticate with Touch ID or password
lock.auth_reason: Unlock Vauchi to access your contacts
lock.subtitle: Authenticate to access your contacts
lock.title: Vauchi is Locked
lock_screen.title: Enter Password
multi_stage.camera_required_detail: Tap Grant Permission to allow camera access for the exchange.
multi_stage.camera_unavailable_detail: This device cannot scan QR codes for exchange.
multi_stage.proximity_not_confirmed_deta: Hold the phones closer together and try again.
multi_stage.qr_broadcast_label: Keep screens facing each other until the other phone finishes
multi_stage.shake_not_confirmed_detail: Shake both phones together at the same time and try again.
my_info.empty_entries:
No entries yet. Add your first entry to share with contacts.

You can add phone numbers, email addresses, websites, social profiles, and more. Tap "Add Entry" to get started.
my_info.empty_groups: No groups created. Create groups to control field visibility.
my_info.exchange_prompt_detail: Share your contact card in person — private and secure.
my_info.preview.title: They see you as
my_info_entry_detail.delete_field_hint: Removes this entry from your contact card. Contacts who already have it keep their copy until their next sync.
my_info_entry_detail.no_contacts: No contacts can see this entry.
notification.duress_alert.title: Duress Alert
notification.emergency_alert.title: Emergency Alert
offline.banner: You're offline. Changes will sync when you reconnect.
onboarding.add_another_device_desc: Move your account to a new phone, tablet, or computer
onboarding.backup_password_subtitle: The password you set when you exported the backup.
onboarding.device_link_guidance_body: To move your account to this device, export a backup on your other signed-in device (Settings → Backup), then restore it here. Live device linking is coming soon.
onboarding.done_subtitle: This is what contacts will see
onboarding.done_title: What would you like to do?
onboarding.groups_custom_a11y_hint: Enter a name for a custom contact group
onboarding.groups_custom_placeholder: Type a group name and press Enter
onboarding.groups_recommendation: Groups are optional, but we strongly recommend them. They let you control exactly who sees what on your card.
onboarding.info_subtitle: Optional — you can add more later.
onboarding.link_device_instructions_link: Tap the link your other device sent you and Vauchi will continue here.
onboarding.link_device_instructions_scan: On your other signed-in device, go to Settings → Linked Devices and tap “Link a device”. Then scan the code with this camera.
onboarding.link_device_instructions_subt: Use your other signed-in device to invite this one.
onboarding.name_a11y_hint: Enter the name others will see on your contact card
onboarding.name_instruction: Enter the name you'd like to show on your contact card.
onboarding.restore_backup_desc: Restore your identity, contacts, and card from an encrypted backup file.
onboarding.restore_link_desc: Scan a QR code on your other device to link this one.
onboarding.restore_subtitle: Choose how to restore your existing identity.
onboarding.restore_transfer_desc: Move all contacts and data from your old device via QR code.
onboarding.welcome_multidevice_desc: Use Vauchi on all your devices with seamless sync.
onboarding.welcome_private_desc: Your data is end-to-end encrypted and never leaves your control.
permission.camera.rationale: Vauchi uses the camera to scan your contact's QR code during an exchange.
permission.camera.title: Camera access needed
places_list.delete_warning: Delete the place "{name}"? Contacts met there keep their coordinates; only the name is removed.
places_list.title: Places
platform.qr_camera_not_available: Camera is unavailable. Paste the QR data instead.
platform.qr_no_camera: No camera detected. Paste the QR data instead.
platform.qr_no_camera_paste: No camera available. Paste the QR data below.
platform.qr_paste_peer_data: Paste the peer's QR code data:
platform.qr_scan_instruction_camera: Position the QR code in front of the camera.
platform.qr_scan_instruction_paste: Hold the QR code up to the camera, or paste the data below.
privacy.data_request_description: Request a copy of all your stored data
privacy.delete.a11y_confirm_hint: Starts a 7-day grace period after which all your data is destroyed. Cannot be undone once the grace period ends.
privacy.delete.contacts_detail: All contact cards and exchange history — permanently deleted
privacy.delete.devices_detail: Other devices will lose access to this identity
privacy.delete.identity_detail: Cryptographic keys and display name — permanently destroyed
privacy.delete.irreversible_detail: After a 7-day grace period, all data is permanently destroyed. You can cancel during the grace period.
privacy.delete.relay_detail: Revocation broadcast sent to contacts, relay blobs purged
privacy.delete_now.detail: All identity, contact, and relay data is destroyed immediately.
privacy.delete_now.subtitle: The grace period has elapsed
privacy.deletion_grace_period: Identity deletion has a 7-day grace period. You can cancel anytime.
privacy.export_description: Download all your data in a portable format
privacy.title: Privacy & Data
privacy.view_data_desc: See what data is stored locally
qr.a11y_multipart_display: Multipart QR code display, showing part {current} of {total}
qr.point_camera: Point your camera at the QR code
qr.scanner_point_hint: Point camera at a Vauchi QR code
rate_limit.message: Please wait a moment before trying again.
rate_limit.title: Too many requests
recovery.claim_expired_detail: This recovery claim is no longer valid. Ask your contact to create a new one.
recovery.claim_meet_instruction: Meet each trusted contact in person. They scan this code to create a voucher for you.
recovery.claim_qr_a11y_hint: Show this to trusted contacts so they can vouch for you
recovery.cli_hint: Use CLI for detailed recovery workflow: vauchi recovery --help
recovery.collect_more_vouchers: Collect {remaining} more voucher(s) from trusted contacts.
recovery.confidence_high_detail: {count} mutual contacts vouched. This looks safe.
recovery.confidence_low_detail: No mutual contacts vouched. Verify this person's identity through another channel before proceeding.
recovery.confidence_medium_detail: {count} mutual contact(s) vouched. Consider verifying before proceeding.
recovery.create_claim_description: Lost your device? Start the recovery process.
recovery.create_claim_instruction: Enter the public key from your lost identity:
recovery.create_hint: Press [c] to create a recovery claim if needed
recovery.enter_old_key_full_instruction: Enter your OLD public key (from backup or previous device). The key is a 64-character hex string.
recovery.enter_old_key_instruction: Enter your OLD public key (from backup or previous device):
recovery.error_no_old_key: Please enter your old public key
recovery.give_claim_instruction: Give this claim to each of your trusted contacts so they can vouch for your recovery.
recovery.give_voucher_detail: Give this voucher to your contact.
recovery.give_voucher_instruction: Give this to the person recovering:
recovery.give_voucher_to_contact: Give this voucher to your contact:
recovery.help_contact_recover_descriptio: If a contact lost their device, you can vouch for their identity.
recovery.help_intro_detail: If a contact lost their device, you can vouch for their identity to help them recover.
recovery.identity_recovered: Your identity has been recovered! Your contacts will now trust your new identity.
recovery.key_invalidated.body: A change to your device's security keys (for example a new fingerprint, a removed lock screen, or a system reset) made your previous Vauchi data unreadable. Your contacts and identity are stored encrypted on this device — without the original key they can't be recovered here.
recovery.key_invalidated.new_identity_bo: Continue with a fresh identity. Previously-exchanged contacts will need to exchange cards with you again. If another device is still linked, you can re-link it from that device's Settings → Linked Devices after onboarding.
recovery.key_invalidated.restore_body: If you previously exported a backup file, you can restore your identity and contacts here.
recovery.key_invalidated.title: Couldn't unlock your data
recovery.linked_devices_hint: You have {count} other linked device(s). If you lost one device but still have another, revoke the lost device from Device Management instead of using recovery.
recovery.lost_device_description: You can recover your contact relationships through social vouching.
recovery.mark_more_trusted: Mark {count} more contact(s) as trusted for recovery before starting the recovery process.
recovery.need_more_vouchers: You need {remaining} more voucher(s). Meet with trusted contacts in person to collect them.
recovery.no_active_claim_description: You don't have an active recovery claim. Start a new recovery process if you need to recover a lost identity.
recovery.not_in_contacts_warning: This key is NOT in your contacts. Verify in person!
recovery.note: Note: Recovery requires mutual verification in person.
recovery.paste_claim_instruction: Paste the recovery claim data from your contact:
recovery.paste_instructions: Paste the recovery claim data your contact shared with you. Verify their identity in person before continuing.
recovery.proof_submitted_detail: Your contacts will be notified. They can accept your new identity to restore your contact relationships.
recovery.risky_accept_warning: No mutual contacts have vouched for {name}. Accepting without verification is risky — this could be an impersonation attempt.
recovery.share_claim_instruction: Share this claim with your trusted contacts:
recovery.share_proof_instruction: Share this proof with your contacts to restore your relationships.
recovery.show_qr_subtitle: Show this QR code to your trusted contacts
recovery.step1: Lost your device? Create a new identity on a new device
recovery.step1_desc: First, create a new identity on your new device.
recovery.step2: Generate a recovery claim with your OLD public key
recovery.step2_desc: Create a claim using your OLD public key from your lost identity.
recovery.step3: Ask 3+ contacts to vouch for you in person
recovery.step3_desc: Meet with 3+ trusted contacts in person. Have them vouch for your recovery.
recovery.step3_desc_original: Meet with trusted contacts in person. Have them vouch for your recovery.
recovery.step4: Collect vouchers to prove you're the same person
recovery.step4_desc: Once you have enough vouchers, share your recovery proof with all contacts.
recovery.step5: Share your recovery proof with all contacts
recovery.threshold_met: Threshold met! You can now recover your identity.
recovery.title: Recovery
recovery.verify_call_instruction: Ask the person to read their old fingerprint aloud over a phone call. If it matches, you can safely vouch.
recovery.verify_in_person_warning: Verify this person's identity IN PERSON before vouching!
recovery.verify_recommendation_high: Multiple contacts you know have vouched. Safe to accept.
recovery.verify_recommendation_low: No known contacts have vouched. Verify identity carefully before accepting.
recovery.verify_recommendation_medium: One contact you know has vouched. Consider verifying in person.
recovery.verify_screen_subtitle: Compare this fingerprint with the contact via phone or text
recovery.vouch_description: Help a contact recover their identity.
recovery.vouch_instruction: Paste the recovery claim from your contact:
recovery.vouch_step1_desc: Meet your contact in person. Verify they are who they claim to be.
recovery.vouch_step2_desc: They will share their claim data with you.
recovery.vouch_step2_desc_alt: They share their recovery claim data with you.
recovery.vouch_step3_desc: Sign a voucher confirming their identity.
recovery.vouch_step4_desc: Give them the voucher data to add to their recovery proof.
recovery.vouch_warning: Only vouch for someone you can verify IN PERSON. This prevents identity theft.
recovery.voucher_created_subtitle: Show this QR code to the recovering contact so they can scan it
recovery.voucher_qr_a11y_hint: The recovering contact scans this to add your voucher
recovery.what_is_not_recovered: NOT recovered: message history, device-specific settings, and trust levels. Your contacts will re-send their cards once they accept your recovery.
recovery.what_is_recovered: What is recovered: contact relationships and the ability to communicate with your contacts.
resistance.duress.confirm_hint: Re-enter the same 6-digit duress PIN to confirm.
resistance.duress.description: Set a secondary PIN that shows decoy contacts and silently alerts trusted contacts
resistance.duress.disable_a11y_hint: Disabling removes silent alert protection for your contacts.
resistance.duress.disable_warning: Disabling the duress PIN removes protection. Alert contacts will no longer be notified.
resistance.duress.enter_hint: Enter a 6-digit PIN that will silently alert your contacts when used.
resistance.duress.error_too_short: PIN must be at least {min} characters
resistance.duress.include_location_desc: Share your location in the alert
resistance.duress.message_placeholder: Message to send when duress PIN is used
resistance.duress.setup.description: Set a secondary PIN that triggers safety measures when entered
resistance.duress.setup.title: Duress PIN Setup
resistance.duress.title: Duress Protection
resistance.duress.what_is_detail: A secondary PIN that, when entered, silently alerts your chosen contacts while appearing to unlock normally.
resistance.emergency.confirm_send: Are you sure? This will alert all trusted contacts.
resistance.emergency.default_message: I may be in danger. Please check on me.
resistance.emergency.description: Send an encrypted alert to trusted contacts
resistance.emergency.title: Emergency Broadcast
resistance.hidden.description: Hidden contacts are invisible in the main list
resistance.hidden.title: Hidden Contacts
resistance.shred.confirm: This will permanently destroy all contacts, identity, and encryption keys. This cannot be undone.
resistance.shred.panic_description: Immediately destroy all data. No grace period. Irreversible.

settings.accessibility_description: Customize the app for your accessibility needs.
settings.advanced_hint: Network, delivery status, and emergency controls
settings.appearance_description: Customize the look and language of the app.
settings.card_updates_hint: Notify me when a contact updates their card
settings.cert_clear_warning: This will disable certificate pinning and allow connections to any valid relay server.
settings.cert_description: Certificate pinning ensures the app only connects to relay servers presenting a specific certificate, preventing man-in-the-middle attacks.
settings.cert_paste_instruction: Paste a certificate in PEM format to enable pinning. This is typically provided by your organization's IT department.
settings.cert_pem_footer: Paste the certificate provided by your organization. It should begin with '-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----'.
settings.cert_pem_invalid: Invalid PEM format. Certificate must begin with '-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----' and end with '-----END CERTIFICATE-----'.
settings.cert_pinning_description: Verify relay server certificates for extra security
settings.cert_warning_cleared: Warning: Clearing the certificate will allow connections to any valid relay server.
settings.contact_added_hint: When enabled, sends a notification when a new contact is added
settings.data_export_description: Download all your data in a portable format
settings.debug_mode_hint: When enabled, shows developer diagnostic information
settings.decoy_contacts_hint: Opens the decoy contact list shown in duress mode
settings.delete_identity_description: Permanently destroy your cryptographic identity and all data
settings.delivery_receipts_hint: When enabled, notifies senders when their messages are delivered
settings.devices_hint: Opens the list of linked devices
settings.display_name_hint: Your visible name shown to contacts
settings.duress_pin_hint: Opens the duress PIN configuration screen
settings.emergency_wipe_confirm_hint: This action is irreversible and will destroy all data
settings.emergency_wipe_confirm_warning: This will permanently delete all data. This action cannot be undone.
settings.emergency_wipe_hint: Permanently deletes all app data. This action cannot be undone
settings.error_invalid_relay_url: URL must start with https:// (or http:// for local dev)
settings.failed_deliveries_hint: Number of updates that failed to deliver
settings.funding_hint: Opens the funding and support page
settings.high_contrast_hint: When enabled, increases color contrast for better visibility
settings.language_hint: Pick the display language of the app
settings.large_touch_targets_hint: When enabled, increases the size of interactive elements
settings.local_encrypted: Your identity is stored locally and encrypted.
settings.new_field_default_a11y: Toggle whether new contact card entries start visible
settings.new_field_default_hint: When on, new contact card entries are shared with all contacts. When off, they stay hidden until you share them.
settings.pending_updates_hint: Number of contact updates waiting to be delivered
settings.reduce_motion_hint: When enabled, minimizes animations and motion effects
settings.relay_help: URL must start with https:// (or http:// for local dev)
settings.relay_url_hint: The relay server used for message delivery
settings.setup_new_device_hint: Transfer your contacts to a new device via QR code

settings.sync_status_hint: Opens the sync status detail screen

shred.panic_detail: All data is cryptographically shredded now, with no grace period.

shred.wipe.contacts_detail: Your contact cards and exchange history will be permanently removed.
